decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.16°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed because it exceeded the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its maximum effective value, the next most impactful lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.84 to ~1.008), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain width of 1.2.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction, reducing thermal resistance and lowering the peak chip temperature. This is the primary geometric lever since material conductivity is already maximized.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved lateral heat distribution across the top layer
risk_notes: Ensure the new width (1.008) plus x0 (0.25) does not exceed the design domain width (1.2); currently 1.258 > 1.2, so this change requires a simultaneous shift in x0 or a smaller width increase.
Correction: The proposed width 1.008 with x0=0.25 results in x1=1.258, which exceeds the domain width of 1.2. I must adjust the proposal to either reduce the width increase or move the spreader left.
